title: Anguina anamalayna
taxonomy: Anguina anamalayna is a species of nematode worm, specifically a type of plant-parasitic nematode. It belongs to the family Anguinidae, which includes various species of nematodes that parasitize plants. Anguina anamalayna is classified as a species within the genus Anguina, which comprises plant-parasitic nematodes. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Further research is needed to fully understand the taxonomic relationships within this genus.
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant species named "Anguina anamalayna". It is possible that this is a misspelling or a name of a plant that is not widely recognized.

However, I found information on a plant species called "Anguina agrostis" which is a type of nematode parasite that infects grasses.
